edus.text: Republican presidential candidate Sen. Ted Cruz has surged past Donald Trump
and opened up a 10-point lead in the early caucus state of Iowa ,
according to a new poll
released Sunday .
Mr. Cruz led the field with 31 percent of the vote among likely Republican caucus-goers in Iowa ,
followed by Mr. Trump with 21 percent ,
according to the Des Moines Register/Bloomberg poll .
The results confirm
that Mr. Cruz has replaced Mr. Trump as the front-runner in the Hawkeye State .
It is the third consecutive survey
to give Mr. Cruz the advantage ,
but also the largest lead yet for the Texas senator ,
who has aggressively courted the state ' s conservative and evangelical voters .
Mr. Cruz led Mr. Trump by two points , 28 percent to 26 percent , in a Fox News poll
released Sunday ,
and by five points , 24 percent to 19 percent , in a Monmouth University poll last week .
In the Des Moines Register/Bloomberg poll , retired neurosurgeon Ben Carson placed third at 13 percent ,
followed by Florida Sen. Marco Rubio at 10 percent and former Florida Gov . Jeb Bush at 6 percent .
The rest of the crowded field garnered low single digits in the poll .
Mr. Trump continues to be out in front in national polls ,
but his lead is shrinking .
The billionaire businessman and reality TV star led nationally with 27 percent ,
followed by Mr. Cruz at 22 percent ,
according to a NBC News/Wall Street Journal poll
released Sunday .
Mr. Rubio placed third at 15 percent ,
followed by Mr. Carson at 11 percent , Mr. Bush at 7 percent and former Hewlett-Packard CEO Carly Fiorina at 5 percent .
The rest finished in the low single digits .
On the Democratic side , former Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton remained the favorite
to win the nomination ,
with double-digit leads nationally and in Iowa .
However , several recent polls have shown Vermont Sen. Bernard Sanders in the lead in the early primary state of New Hampshire .
He led Mrs. Clinton by 10 points , 50 percent to 40 percent , in a CNN/WMUR poll last week in the Granite State .
